meta.pageTitle: Open Framework for Artists · AI Pathways Map

siteHeader.brandSubtitle: An open framework for protection, care, and experimentation

hero.title: Becoming Digital Ancestors
hero.bodyBeforeProject: We will all be ancestors one day. Pick your stance, and interact with our map as a guardian, steward, or trailblazer.

why.title: Why we care
why.body: Generative AI is reshaping how art is made, circulated, and valued. It can bring up grief, frustration, curiosity, and possibility all at the same time. This project brings together practicing artists, AI researchers, technologists, and policy advisors who care about artist autonomy and responsible technology.

storyCard.intro: This map grew out of months of creative research and development experiments in
storyCard.middle: . It started with a question: Can AI help care for creative legacy, and cultural memory? We designed gallery works, kept process journals, and tested a number of tools over six months. Alongside our art exhibit, we turned what we learned into an
storyCard.middleSuffix: that other artists can adapt, critique, and help build upon.
storyCard.closing: The map helps clarify what you might want from your own engagement with AI, from opting out entirely to collaborating on new work.

who.title: Arts communities and the people around them
who.body: The framework is designed for working artists and creative practitioners. It is also useful for cultural workers, arts organizations, educators, policy-makers, and AI builders who want to better understand artist needs.

artists.heading: For artists and creative professionals
artists.body: Clarify your boundaries, learn practical safety steps, and explore new workflows if you want them. Use the map when posting work, signing agreements, applying for funding, or testing new tools.

organisations.heading: For arts and legal organisations
organisations.body: Use the framework in workshops, office hours, or resource hubs. The map can support legal clinics, digital safety training, and collective discussions.

policy.heading: For policy makers
policy.body: See where artists encounter friction in current tools and contracts. Use the map to surface real-world scenarios when shaping policy.

researchers.heading: For AI researchers and developers
researchers.body: Treat the map as a set of user stories that highlight artist concerns around consent, provenance, and creative control.

cards.label: Guardian
cards.title: Reduce how much work is used for AI training
cards.body: For artists who want to minimise how much of their work ends up in training datasets or is copied by AI models.
items: Image cloaking tools like Glaze or Nightshade
Contract language for client and platform agreements

cards.label: Steward
cards.title: Use AI for studio admin without oversharing
cards.body: For artists who want to use AI as a studio assistant. For example, help with writing and planning while keeping control over their data.
items: Mainstream and local chat tool options

cards.label: Trailblazer
cards.title: Make new work with AI
cards.body: For artists experimenting with AI media who want to stay grounded in ethics and transparency.
items: Model choices and data sourcing basics
Train models with your own data
What current copyright law covers and what it does not

map.title: Click through decisions and receive guided actions

siteFooter.outro: project in collaboration with artists and responsible AI practitioners. This is a living framework (v23).
